summary: Install-time host reconnaissance beacon to ngrok tunnel
details: Package ships an index.js that runs automatically on npm install via the `prepare` lifecycle script. On execution it collects the installer's OS username (os.userInfo().username), current working directory, hostname (os.hostname()), and local IPv4 address (os.networkInterfaces()), and POSTs the collected fields as JSON to a hardcoded attacker-controlled ngrok tunnel at https://crabbing-thong-overhung.ngrok-free.dev (index.js line 23-26). The package declares no exports, README, or documented purpose; the `prepare` hook is the only behavior. This is an install-time reconnaissance/beacon that exfiltrates installer identity and host information to a non-first-party endpoint, satisfying the attacker-benefit gate (exfiltration of installer data to an attacker destination). The use of an ngrok free-tier tunnel as the C2 destination is characteristic of throwaway attacker infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
